hints: Because k is uniform on 0..8, each digit is equally likely a 1 or a 9.
solution: Choosing $k$ uniformly makes each digit symmetric between $1$ and $9$, so $\mathbb{E}[\text{digit}]=5$. Hence $\mathbb{E}[X]=5(10^7+\cdots+1)=5\cdot11111111=55555555.$

hints: A first flip can't win; from each player's second flip on, they complete the pair w.p. 1/2.
From turn 3 it's alternating Bernoulli(1/2) trials, Alice first.
solution: Nobody can win on a first flip; thereafter each own-flip completes the H-and-T pair with probability $\tfrac12$. From Alice's second flip onward the winners alternate as Bernoulli$(\tfrac12)$ trials with Alice first: $P=\tfrac12+\tfrac14P$, giving $P=\tfrac23.$

hints: There are 6! labelings, but the rotation group of a cube has 24 elements.
solution: There are $6!$ ways to assign the numbers, but $24$ rotations of the cube identify equivalent labelings, so the count is $\dfrac{6!}{24}=\dfrac{720}{24}=30.$

hints: You must draw 5 real bills in a row (a fake resets progress); each draw is real w.p. 1/2.
Expected draws for k consecutive successes = (p^{−k}−1)/(1−p).
solution: You need $5$ real bills in a row, each real with probability $\tfrac12$; a fake resets. The expected number of draws for $k$ consecutive successes is $\dfrac{p^{-k}-1}{1-p}=\dfrac{2^5-1}{1/2}=62.$

hints: The expected number of k-cycles in a random permutation is 1/k.
solution: A random permutation has $\tfrac1k$ expected $k$-cycles, so $\mathbb{E}[C_n]=\sum_{k=n+1}^{2n}\tfrac1k\to\ln(2n)-\ln(n)=\ln2$. Thus $q=2.$

hints: Fix X₁; P(nearest arc L≥x)=(1−x/π)^{19} for x∈(0,π).
solution: Fixing $X_1$, no other point lies within arc $x$ on either side with probability $(1-\tfrac{x}{\pi})^{19}$, so $\mathbb{E}[L]=\int_0^\pi(1-\tfrac{x}{\pi})^{19}dx=\tfrac{\pi}{20}$ radians $=\tfrac{180}{20}=9^\circ.$

hints: Each $5 adds change, each $10 needs change: it's a balanced-ballot (Dyck path) condition.
solution: The cashier can always make change iff in every prefix the number of \$5 bills is at least the number of \$10 bills — a balanced ballot condition. With $n=19$ of each, the probability is the Catalan ratio $\dfrac1{n+1}=\dfrac1{20}.$
The Kelly criterion gives $f^*=p-\dfrac{q}{b}=0.75-\dfrac{0.25}{1}=0.5$, i.e. bet $50\%$ of the bankroll.

hints: Replicate: long the underlying, short the K=25 call.
solution: Since $\min(S_T,25)=S_T-\max(S_T-25,0)$, replicate by holding the underlying and shorting the $K=25$ call: price $=S_0-C_{25}=21-0.7=20.3.$
By Bayes, $P(\text{double}\mid n\text{ heads})=\dfrac{1}{1+19/2^n}$. Requiring $\ge\tfrac{19}{20}$ gives $2^n>361$, so the minimum is $n=9.$

hints: The forward replicates as long underlying minus K zero-coupon bonds.
solution: A forward is replicated by holding the asset and borrowing the present value of $K$: price $=S_0-Ke^{-rT}=3-2e^{-0.04}\approx3-1.9216=1.08.$
